content.claim: Poland's 2017 reform reverting from 6+3+3 back to 8+4 structure (re-lengthening primary, abolishing gimnazjum) incurred large implementation costs: approximately 7,000 junior high schools were closed, up to 10,000 teachers faced job losses, and in the double-cohort transition year, 3,173 students in Warsaw alone did not secure school placement
content.context: Fresh live research through the Polish system reform lens
limitations: Documents the transition shock but does not establish whether these costs were specific to the direction of reversal or inherent to any major structural reorganization
Does not measure whether similar transition costs would occur in a comprehensive-direction reform
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
